completed_since_last_snapshot: Ran the full Reboot Lifecycle at session start: /jump-once-protocol + /jump-once-task + /level-design-principle invoked; meta/handoff_latest.json read; pkg_hash s9-f2a9d7 confirmed matching the PKG at session start; npm test confirmed 711/711 green before any change. Confirmed the S9.6–S9.11 work the prior handoff flagged as uncommitted had already been committed by the user as e88629b before this session started — no commit/push action was needed or taken this session.

Authored the P10 — Content Generation section of docs/execution_plan.md via a first-principles adversarial review grounded in a direct reading of src/gen/Pipeline.ts, Concept.ts, Creativity.ts, IntentGate.ts, GenProfile.ts; src/eval/Evaluate.ts, AgentHarness.ts; src/eval/local/Optimization.ts, Solvability.ts, Softlock.ts; src/eval/campaign/CampaignDirector.ts, CampaignState.ts, MechanicTracker.ts; src/eval/macro/Curriculum.ts; src/eval/gdos/DesignSpace.ts, Economy.ts, Emotional.ts (delegated to an Explore subagent for the raw reads, synthesized directly here — zero-assumption, no signature guessed).

The review found: (1) four REQs P10 owns (REQ-084, REQ-174's P10 share, REQ-050(applied), and the P10 remainders of REQ-012/090/091/101/102) mapped to no slice in the naive four-slice table; (2) the naive S10.2 ('dual-path level generation') was sized as if generating the ENTIRE campaign fits one session; (3) zero schema exists anywhere for the REQ-083 seven-step chapter framework or for a persisted campaign/chapter data payload; (4) zero code path turns a PipelineProduct into a LevelRecord — REQ-090's prose 'Campaign Integration' phase 8 corresponds to no code (PipelinePhase has 7 members, not 8; manufactureLevel's accept branch just returns the product and stops).

Ten decisions ledgered dm-0108–dm-0114 (design_memory_ledger.json, now 114 decisions total, JSON-validated): dm-0108 a new one-way-isolated content/ subtree holds authored/generated campaign data (mirrors src/gen/, tools/, render/'s own isolation treatment — no existing subtree was a correct home); dm-0109 content/schema/ChapterFramework.ts is the REQ-083 eighth versioned strict-parsed schema, with 'framework before generation' enforced STRUCTURALLY (buildConceptFromSlot is the only path into manufactureLevel, and it requires an already-parsed framework slot) rather than by convention; dm-0110 REQ-100 Dual-Path is proven by COMPOSITION over the existing OptimizationVerdict (applicable && deltaSeconds >= threshold) — explicitly NOT a new path-counting mechanism or literal alternate geometry (that would violate REQ-005's rejection of power-set depth expansion); dm-0111 REQ-084 difficulty tiers are derived from MEASURED evidence (TierTimes.discovery + attempt counts + deltaSeconds via a calibrated DifficultyProfile), never substituted from the authored difficultyTarget intent vector — three distinct pre-existing 'difficulty/tier' concepts (TierTimes routing tiers, difficultyVectors authored intent, CurriculumLevel.difficulty unspecified scalar) were found and explicitly NOT conflated; dm-0112 content/Assembler.ts::integrateProduct is the literal, previously-unbuilt REQ-090 phase 8, built as NEW P10 code (not a P7 retrofit — P7 is VERIFIED and closed on exactly its 7 phases per dm-0064); dm-0113 full-campaign GDOS/macro-curriculum evaluation runs via a separate `npm run validate:campaign` script (tools/sdlc/validateCampaign.js, not yet built) writing content/data/validation-report.json, keeping npm test's runtime content-volume-independent (one pilot-chapter smoke regression only); dm-0114 content generation uses overgenerate-and-curate (Creativity.evolveLevel's existing REQ-081 loop selects the highest evaluateLevel-scoring candidate per slot, code-enforced, ledgered) as the PRIMARY authoring path — the P8/P9 editor is a touch-up path only, and any touch-up output must still pass evaluateLevel before acceptance.

docs/task_slices.md Phase 10 table restructured from 4 slices to 6 (S10.1 content schema + content/ subtree + whole-campaign chapter architecture; S10.2 difficulty estimator + dual-path proof + integration assembler, unit-proven against fixtures only; S10.3 pilot chapter end-to-end with real content + a GDOS calibration checkpoint; S10.4 batch production of remaining chapters; S10.5 campaign assembly + macro validation + REQ-084 enforcement + REQ-174 wiring closure; S10.6 verification report + M5 closure). Phase 11's S11.2 dependency updated from S10.4 to S10.6 to match. critical_warnings_for_next_session: RUN THE REBOOT LIFECYCLE FIRST: confirm pkg_hash s10-b7c1e4 matches meta/project_knowledge_graph.json; npm test expect 711 passing (this session added zero tests — it was planning-only, no code).

P10's execution plan is AUTHORED but UNIMPLEMENTED. Do not skip S10.1's own PLAN/ANCHOR stages just because docs/execution_plan.md's P10 section already exists — that section is the phase-level plan (REQ-P02); each slice still needs its own per-slice PLAN stage per jump-once-task's nine-stage loop.

CAMPAIGN SIZE IS NOT YET DECIDED. The execution plan deliberately does NOT fix a chapter or level-slot count (finding 8 of the adversarial review) — it must be derived at S10.1 from src/gen/Lifecycle.ts's live mechanic registry (one 'introduce' chapter per not-yet-retired teachable mechanic family, plus at minimum one combination chapter and one mastery/endgame chapter). Do not invent a round number without reading that registry first.

THREE PRE-EXISTING 'DIFFICULTY/TIER' CONCEPTS MUST NOT BE CONFLATED (dm-0111): TierTimes (Optimization.ts's discovery/good/fast/expert/worldRecord — per-level ROUTING tiers, REQ-101), DifficultyAxis/difficultyVectors (LevelConcept's authored [0,1] INTENT vector, provisional since dm-0015, never validated against outcomes), and CurriculumLevel.difficulty (an unspecified scalar the caller supplies). The NEW content/DifficultyEstimator.ts's DifficultyTier (Easy/Medium/Hard/Harder/Very-Hard, REQ-084's campaign bucket) is a FOURTH, distinct concept derived from MEASURED evidence only — never read difficultyTarget as a level's shipped tier.

REQ-100 DUAL-PATH MUST NOT BECOME LITERAL ALTERNATE GEOMETRY (dm-0110). It is proven by composition over OptimizationVerdict.applicable && deltaSeconds >= profile.dualPath.minDeltaSeconds — a level's execution-quality routes through IDENTICAL geometry, not branching layout. Authoring real alternate physical routes per level would violate REQ-005's explicit rejection of power-set depth expansion.

REQ-090'S 'CAMPAIGN INTEGRATION' PHASE 8 DOES NOT EXIST IN src/gen/Pipeline.ts (confirmed by direct read — PipelinePhase has exactly 7 members). content/Assembler.ts::integrateProduct (dm-0112) is NEW P10 code that closes this gap; it is not a P7 retrofit, and src/gen/Pipeline.ts itself should NOT be reopened to add an eighth PipelinePhase enum value — P7 is VERIFIED and closed on its own 7 phases (dm-0064), and adding campaign-shaped logic to src/gen/ would violate its own isolation rule (gen/ reads campaign/ as types only, dm-0057).

FULL-CAMPAIGN VALIDATION BELONGS IN `npm run validate:campaign` (tools/sdlc/validateCampaign.js, NOT YET BUILT — dm-0113), NOT in npm test. Do not add per-level evaluateLevel calls as individual npm test cases as chapters are batch-produced at S10.4 — that would silently regress npm test's runtime back to content-volume-coupled, undoing the explicit design decision. npm test should only ever gain: schema parse/reject tests, estimator/dual-path/assembler unit tests against fixtures, and exactly ONE full-pipeline smoke regression from the S10.3 pilot chapter.

OVERGENERATE-AND-CURATE (dm-0114) IS THE PRIMARY CONTENT-AUTHORING PATH, NOT THE P8/P9 EDITOR. The editor is a touch-up path only for a near-miss candidate; any touch-up output must still re-enter evaluateLevel before acceptance — there is no bypass. Candidate selection (highest evaluateLevel score in Creativity.evolveLevel's pool) must be code-enforced in content/Assembler.ts, never a manual/session-level choice, to keep REQ-050 ('all creative decisions originate from GDOS') true by construction.

S10.3'S PILOT CHAPTER IS A CALIBRATION CHECKPOINT, NOT JUST A CONTENT MILESTONE. GDOS/macro-curriculum thresholds have ONLY ever been tested against synthetic fixtures through P4–P6 (dm-0031's profile was never run against real content). If the pilot chapter fails a threshold, the correct response is a LEDGERED CDRE profile-evolution recalibration (P5's own designed mechanism) — NEVER a per-level threshold override, which the Definition of Done explicitly prohibits. Do not proceed to S10.4's batch production until the pilot's thresholds hold or are properly recalibrated and ledgered.

content/ IS A NEW ONE-WAY-ISOLATED SUBTREE (dm-0108), same discipline as src/gen/ (dm-0057), tools/ (dm-0066), render/ (dm-0081). It may import src/gen/ and src/eval/ ONLY through their named public seams (manufactureLevel, Concept.validateConcept, Creativity.evolveLevel; evaluateLevel/assembleLevelEvidence, AgentHarness.runAgent/CANONICAL_PIPELINE, Optimization.computeOptimizationWindow, campaign/'s processCampaign/updateState, macro/Curriculum.validateCurriculum). Nothing under src/, tools/, or render/ may ever import content/. test/unit/ContentIsolation.test.ts (not yet created — build it at S10.1 first, before any other content/ file, following the established pattern) is what will enforce this.

Do not assume the shape.
